page: Microsoft Azure D48pls_v5 is an ARM-based virtual machine belonging to the DPLSv5 server family. It is powered by an Ampere Altra ARMv8 Neoverse-N1 processor operating at 3.0 GHz. The instance features 48 dedicated physical cores and 48 vCPUs, meaning there is no hyperthreading, resulting in 1.0 thread per core. It is configured with 96.0 GB of RAM, yielding a resource density of 2.0 GB of memory per core. The CPU cache configuration includes 64 KB of L1d cache, 1.0 MB of L2 cache, and 32 MB of L3 cache. This server does not include local storage or integrated GPUs, but it is premium storage capable.

In benchmark testing, the D48pls_v5 exhibits strong multi-core performance, as demonstrated by a stress-ng multi-core CPU score of 84952.55, which falls into the top 25% tier. This is complemented by strong Redis SET operations performance, reaching 60.51 million operations per second. However, single-core capabilities are more moderate; the single-core stress-ng score is 1822.69, and the Geekbench single-core score of 1084.0 is categorized in the bottom 25%. Memory bandwidth is stable, with read-only small block cached speeds at 3314.63 GB/sec and large block uncached write speeds at 77.04 GB/sec. Large-model LLM inference using a 70B model is weak at 5.3 tokens/sec for prompt processing, though small-model 135M text generation reaches an average of 117.58 tokens/sec.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the D48pls_v5 offers a high density of physical ARM cores relative to its memory footprint, making it a viable choice for multi-threaded workloads that do not require massive RAM allocations. The primary tradeoff is the lower single-threaded performance and the lack of bundled local storage. This server is well-suited for highly parallelized workloads, multi-threaded applications, static web serving, software compilation, and database operations that can leverage its 48 dedicated physical cores.
description: The Microsoft Azure D48pls_v5 is an ARM-based server from the DPLSv5 family, featuring 48 dedicated physical cores on an Ampere Neoverse-N1 processor running at 3.0 GHz. It is configured with 96.0 GB of RAM, providing a 2.0 GB per core ratio, and does not include local storage or GPUs. Benchmarks show strong multi-core capabilities, with a stress-ng multi-core score of 84952.55 and Redis SET performance of 60.51 million operations per second, though single-core performance is relatively weak. This resource profile makes it a cost-efficient option for multi-threaded, parallelized workloads such as software compilation, static web serving, and database operations that benefit from dedicated physical cores without requiring high memory-to-CPU ratios.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D48pls_v5 is an ARM-based server featuring 48 dedicated Ampere Neoverse-N1 cores, 96 GB of RAM, and strong multi-core performance suited for parallelized workloads and database operations.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure D48pls_v5 is an ARM-based server with 48 dedicated Ampere cores and 96 GB of RAM, optimized for multi-threaded workloads.
tagline: An ARM-powered server featuring forty-eight dedicated physical cores and ninety-six gigabytes of memory for highly parallelized multi-threaded workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by an Ampere Neoverse-N1 ARMv8 processor at 3.0 GHz
Features 48 dedicated physical cores with no hyperthreading
Equipped with 96.0 GB of RAM (2.0 GB per core)
Delivers strong multi-core performance and Redis throughput
Lacks local storage but supports premium external storage
Best suited for multi-threaded applications and static web serving
